tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Great Lakes smallmouth in heavy wind: crank a 5XD in Green Gizzard Shad on 17lb fluoro. The heavier line reduces running depth — keep bait above the fish, not digging bottom. Speed is the trigger, not bottom deflections. Reel fast, add erratic starts/stops/jerks. Drift with the wind using trolling motor drift mode, make long downwind casts.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Wind and waves make light-line finesse nearly impossible on big water — can't feel bites with bow bouncing and line bowing. That's when crankbaits shine. Use 360 and forward-facing sonar to define bottom composition and find fish. Drift with the wind to conserve battery, make long downwind casts. Use electronics to find irregularities, then cover water with crankbaits.'
